headline: Claude Fable 5 dominated the week as agentic development tooling broke out across coding workflows.
body: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 anchored the window, drawing tutorials on Claude Code as a systematic coding platform and serious backlash over aggressive safety filters that refuse harmless prompts and raise fallback-model suspicions. AI-assisted development, KV cache quantization, RLHF, and state stacks all registered as breakouts, while vector database, AI engineering, browser automation, and agent memory led the tool rotation. Cursor described orchestrating 128 coding agents at once with inter-agent communication, and creators showed end-to-end automation pipelines built on Fable 5. New entrants ASIC accelerators, text-to-CAD, and faithful generation appeared at the edges.
